threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’ve still got your Tesco bags—local knows you should be hitting the markets for your fruit and veg by now.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham and overheard a guy trying to convince his mates that the bus takes the scenic route. It's just dark and quiet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the kebab guy at my corner spot remembers my order and chats about the weather like we’re old friends. That’s when I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a hidden club in an old factory where the toilets are art installations. It's wild and strangely charming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can spot a tourist from a block away by their overly glossy map, but still help them find the best burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a man serenading commuters at the Civic Center stop yesterday—full opera voice, no shame. Only in this city do you get that kind of entertainment on your way to work.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume the Gràcia Festa Major is just a party—it's really a labor of love for the locals, months of planning and creativity.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they still ask for a bottle of water in restaurants instead of just sipping from the tap. It's a little tell that makes me smile.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus will be on time. Real locals know it’s all tea and patience, especially on a Saturday morning.
Caught a glimpse of a bloke playing a tuba on the N68 last night. Only in Brixton would someone turn a bus ride into a jazz gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a marching band practicing for Carnival in the park while the smell of grilled kebabs wafted from the street stalls.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a café that serves coffee brewed from beans ground in a 1920s vintage grinder. It's a time capsule in a cup.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they defend the taco truck on 24th while ignoring the line at La Taqueria. It's a whole vibe.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a group of tourists try to figure out the Muni map while right next to a driver playing the trumpet. Classic SF chaos in motion.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's just about Gaudí and plazas, but the best vibe is in the quiet corners where the old bars still hum with regulars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the Vermut rituals; they always look puzzled when locals cheerfully order their midday drink.
